title: Charmed Life — 4 (Fragment 1)
sourceText:
Chapter Four

4

T 
 HE SAME SOFTNESS and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat the next morning and told him it was time to get up. Bright morning sunshine was flooding the curved walls of his room. Though Cat knew now that the Castle must be full of people, he could hear not a sound from any of them. Nor could he hear anything from outside the windows.

I know what it’s like! Cat thought. It’s like when it’s snowed in the night. The idea made him feel so pleased and so warm that he went to sleep again.

“You really must get up, Eric,” Mary said, shaking him. “I’ve run your bath, and your lessons start at nine. Make haste, or you won’t have time for breakfast.”

Cat got up. He had so strong a feeling that it had snowed in the night that he was quite surprised to find his room warm in the sun. He looked out of the windows, and there were green lawns and flowers, and rooks circling the green trees, as if there had been some mistake. Mary had gone. Cat was glad, because he was not at all sure he liked her, and he was afraid of missing breakfast. When he was dressed, he went along to the bathroom and let the hot water out of the bath. Then he dashed down the twisting stairs to find Gwendolen.

“Where do we go for breakfast?” he asked her anxiously.

Gwendolen was never at her best in the morning. She was sitting on her blue velvet stool in front of her garlanded mirror, crossly combing her golden hair. Combing her hair was another thing which always made her cross. “I don’t know and I don’t care! Shut up!” she said.

“Now that’s no way to speak,” said the maid called Euphemia, briskly following Cat into the room. She was rather a pretty girl, and she did not seem to find her name the burden it should have been. “We’re waiting to give you breakfast along here. Come on.”

Gwendolen hurled her comb down expressively and they followed Euphemia to a room just along the corridor. It was a square, airy room, with a row of big windows but, compared with the rest of the Castle, it was rather shabby. The leather chairs were battered. The grassy carpet had stains on it. None of the cupboards would shut properly. Things like clockwork trains and tennis rackets bulged out. Julia and Roger were sitting waiting at a table by the windows, in clothes as shabby as the room.

Mary, who was waiting there too, said, “And about time!” and began to work an interesting lift in a cupboard by the fireplace. There was a clank. Mary opened the lift and fetched out a large plate of bread and butter and a steaming brown jug of cocoa. She brought these over to the table and Euphemia poured each child a mug of the cocoa.

Gwendolen stared from her mug to the plate of bread. “Is this all there is?”

“What else do you want?” asked Euphemia.

Gwendolen could not find words to express what she wanted. Porridge, bacon and eggs, grapefruit, toast and kippers all occurred to her at once, and she went on staring.

words.definition: The quality of being soft to the touch or gentle.
words.example: The same softness and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat.
words.exampleText: The same softness and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat.

words.definition: Complete absence of sound.
words.example: The same softness and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat.
words.exampleText: The same softness and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat.

words.definition: To fill or cover a place with water or light.
words.example: Bright morning sunshine was flooding the curved walls of his room.
words.exampleText: Bright morning sunshine was flooding the curved walls of his room.

words.definition: Shaped with a smooth, rounded line instead of straight lines.
words.example: Bright morning sunshine was flooding the curved walls of his room.
words.exampleText: Bright morning sunshine was flooding the curved walls of his room.

words.definition: A large building or group of buildings with thick walls, often built in the past to protect people from attack.
words.example: He knew now that the Castle must be full of people.
words.exampleText: He knew now that the Castle must be full of people.

words.definition: Unable to leave a place because snow has blocked the way.
words.example: It’s like when it’s snowed in the night.
words.exampleText: It’s like when it’s snowed in the night.

words.definition: Feeling happy or satisfied.
words.example: The idea made him feel so pleased and so warm that he went to sleep again.
words.exampleText: The idea made him feel so pleased and so warm that he went to sleep again.

words.definition: Speed in doing something, especially because you need to be somewhere soon.
words.example: Make haste, or you won’t have time for breakfast.
words.exampleText: Make haste, or you won’t have time for breakfast.

words.definition: Feeling or showing surprise because something unexpected has happened.
words.example: He was quite surprised to find his room warm in the sun.
words.exampleText: He was quite surprised to find his room warm in the sun.

words.definition: Areas of grass that are kept cut and healthy.
words.example: He looked out of the windows, and there were green lawns and flowers.
words.exampleText: He looked out of the windows, and there were green lawns and flowers.

words.definition: A type of black bird related to crows.
words.example: Rooks circling the green trees.
words.exampleText: Rooks circling the green trees.

words.definition: An action or judgment that is not correct.
words.example: As if there had been some mistake.
words.exampleText: As if there had been some mistake.

words.definition: In a worried or nervous way.
words.example: He asked her anxiously where to go for breakfast.
words.exampleText: He asked her anxiously where to go for breakfast.

words.definition: In an angry or annoyed manner.
words.example: Gwendolen was sitting crossly combing her golden hair.

words.definition: Decorated with a wreath or string of flowers, leaves, or ribbons.
words.example: She sat on her blue velvet stool in front of her garlanded mirror.
words.exampleText: She sat on her blue velvet stool in front of her garlanded mirror.

words.definition: In a quick, energetic way.
words.example: The maid called Euphemia followed Cat briskly into the room.

words.definition: To throw something with force.
words.example: Gwendolen hurled her comb down expressively.

words.definition: In a way that clearly shows feelings or meaning.

words.exampleText: She hurled her comb down expressively.

words.definition: Having lots of space and light; well ventilated.
words.example: It was a square, airy room with big windows.
words.exampleText: It was a square, airy room with big windows.

words.definition: In poor condition through long use or lack of care.
words.example: Compared with the rest of the Castle, it was rather shabby.

words.definition: Damaged by blows or hard use.

words.definition: Marks that are difficult to remove and spoil the appearance of something.

words.exampleText: The grassy carpet had stains on it.

words.definition: A mechanism driven by a spring, often in toys or watches.

words.definition: To convey a thought or feeling in words or gestures.
